reasoning:
Based on recent news up to November 2025, the underlying reference value for 'Riots' in the British Indian Ocean Territory is exactly 1.0 (indicating 0 riots have occurred there in the preceding 360 days). For this question to resolve positively, ACLED would need to record more than 10.0 (i.e. at least 11) distinct 'Riots' in the 30 days prior to each resolution date. 

BIOT consists almost entirely of a secure joint US/UK military base (Diego Garcia) and historically uninhabited outer islands. The only notable civilian population that had the potential to protest—around 60 Tamil asylum seekers—were relocated to the United Kingdom by December 2024. Additionally, although the UK signed a treaty handing sovereignty of the Chagos Islands to Mauritius in May 2025, the actual resettlement of Chagossians involves peaceful, long-term infrastructure building on empty islands, not violent clashes. There is zero evidence of any ongoing civil unrest on the islands, nor is there a sizeable civilian population capable of sustaining 11 distinct riot events within a single month. 

Because the probability of such an extreme spike in violence in an unpopulated military outpost is virtually zero, the probabilities are set to the lowest permissible bounds while slightly escalating over time to reflect the growing, albeit minuscule, uncertainty of the longer forecast horizon.
